general_info.definition: Nitrogen and sulfur co-doped carbon dots (N,S-CDs) derived from egg-white proteins

method.conditions: egg-white freeze-drieded and grinded; egg-white powder and 35mL of reduced graphene oxide ultrasonically treated for 1h; kept at 250°C for 12h;  annealed at 800°C for 2h under N2 gas flow at a rate of 10°C min−1

source: Graphene oxide (35 mL, 4 mg/mL aqueous solution)

post_treatment: Dispersion in diluted HCl and stirring overnight
Vacuum filtration and washing to pH 7

structural.morphology: anchored on the surface of graphene
structural.crystallinity: (100) crystal face of graphite and graphitic crystallites

applications.performance: Composite (N,S-CDs/rGO@S65) delivers 1550 mAh g-1 at 0.5 C; retains 427.9 mAh g⁻¹ after 600 cycles at 5 C
applications.comparative_advantage: Significantly higher rate capability and high sulfur utilization
